articles.title: Heatwave alert in three district of Himachal - Daily Excelsior
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat") Himachal Pradesh
articles.full_text:
SHIMLA, Mar 8 : The Meteorological Centre, Shimla, on Sunday issued a heatwave warning for Kangra, Kullu and Mandi districts for the next two days. Although maximum temperatures have witnessed a slight decline during the past 24 hours, they continue to remain above normal across many parts of Himachal Pradesh.
According to the Meteorological Department, weather in the state is expected to remain mainly dry during the next two days. However, isolated places may experience light rain or thunderstorms under the influence of western disturbances and jet stream winds affecting the Western Himalayan region.
Despite the marginal dip in day temperatures, several lower and mid-hill areas are still experiencing unusually warm conditions for early March. Residents have been advised to take precautions during afternoon hours as heatwave conditions may prevail at isolated places in the districts under warning.
Minimum temperatures across many stations also remain above normal, resulting in warmer-than-usual nights in several parts of the state.In Lahaul and Spiti district, weather conditions remained clear and no weather-related disruption was reported. However, key high-altitude routes including Rohtang Pass to Sarchu and Darcha to Sarchu remain closed due to seasonal conditions, while stretches such as Sumdo-Lossar are open for traffic.
The rainfall outlook for the next 48 hours indicates very low chances of precipitation in Lahaul and Spiti, with hazy sunshine to partly cloudy conditions expected in areas including Keylong, Udaipur and Kaza.Maximum temperatures recorded in the state continue to remain high for the season.
Una recorded the highest maximum temperature at 33.4 C, followed by Mandi at 32.6 C. Bhuntar and Kangra both recorded 31.4 C, while Dharamshala registered 30.4 C and Solan 30.0 C. Jubberhatti airport recorded 27.5 C, Nahan 27.4 C, Chamba 25.7 C and Kasauli 25.1 C. Manali recorded 24.0 C, Kalpa 21.8 C, Shimla 18.9 C and Tabo 16.2 C.
The Meteorological Department said temperatures in several lower hill districts are currently 5 to 7 degrees above normal, indicating an early spell of heat in parts of the state.
(UNI)

articles.title: Yellow heatwave alert issued in Himachal today: Weather likely to change from tomorrow; 4 days of rain & sn... - Bhaskar English
articles.search_term: heatwave (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una)
articles.full_text:
Yellow heatwave alert issued in Himachal today:Weather likely to change from tomorrow; 4 days of rain & snowfall forecast
- Copy link
Heat has started intensifying in Himachal Pradesh in the early days of March. The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for a heatwave in Kangra, Kullu, and Mandi districts for today and has advised people to take precautions.
However, a Western Disturbance is expected to become active from tomorrow, which may bring relief from the heat through rain and snowfall.
Average maximum temperature is 7.5°C
At present, the state’s average maximum temperature is 7.5°C above normal, while the minimum temperature is 5°C above normal. In Keylong (Lahaul-Spiti), the maximum temperature reached 14.7°C, which is 11.7°C above normal.
In the capital Shimla, the maximum temperature touched 24.7°C, about 8.9°C above normal, while Kalpa recorded 21.8°C, which is 11.2°C above normal. Meanwhile, temperatures in 13 cities of the state have crossed 30°C, with Una recording the highest at 33.4°C.
Rain-snowfall for the next 4 days
Thunderstorm alert on 11-12
The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms in Chamba, Kangra, Kullu, and Mandi districts on March 11 and 12. Meanwhile, strong winds are likely in Lahaul-Spiti and Kinnaur districts at a speed of 40 to 50 kilometers per hour.
After this change in weather, there will be a slight drop in temperature in the state, and people may get considerable relief from the current heat.
